# context: Technical discussion about software development, DevOps, and AI agents.
#
# This is the BUNDLED default vocabulary that ships with c3. Keep it
# generic — terms here bias every public installer's STT.
#
# To add your own personal/project terms (product names, internal jargon,
# common misheard words), DO NOT edit this file. Create:
#
#     ~/.config/c3/stt-vocabulary.txt
#
# (or set $C3_STT_VOCAB to point anywhere). The STT pipeline checks the
# user override path first; if present, it REPLACES this default entirely.
# See plugins/c3/stt/stt-pkg/README.md for the format.
#
# Standing instruction for agents using c3: when the user notices an STT
# mistake (especially on names, product names, or jargon), prompt them
# to add the term to ~/.config/c3/stt-vocabulary.txt so the next
# transcription gets it right.

# Tech terms — common shell + infra tooling
tmux != T mux, tea mux
systemctl != system control, system CTL
journalctl != journal control
kubectl != cube control, cube CTL
nginx != engine X
Ed25519 != ED 25519
gRPC != G R P C
WebSocket != web socket
